recent_calls.summary: The user called back the after-hours assistant but ended the call shortly after without specifying any service request or issue. The agent greeted the user and offered assistance, but the call ended abruptly.

Paul called to request an updated quote for a furnace replacement as his previous quote had expired. The agent confirmed his address and contact details, documented the request, and informed him that a team member would call back on the next business day. The call ended with no further requests from Paul.
The call was very brief and did not progress beyond initial greetings. The user and agent exchanged a few words but no meaningful conversation or task completion occurred.

The call was an inbound after-hours call to Budget's assistant, Evie, but the user did not engage with the agent and instead played automated voicemail options repeatedly before hanging up. The agent attempted to assist but was unable to complete any service or information exchange.

Jamie called after hours to report that a dispatch completed last week did not fix the issue with their AC, specifically the evaporator coil replacement. The agent documented the problem and informed Jamie that a team member would call back on the next business day. Jamie confirmed the callback number and declined further assistance.

The user, Latria Guyton, called to follow up on a service request for a leak in her AC unit. The agent confirmed the details and scheduled the earliest possible regular service appointment without an email for updates. The user declined to provide feedback and ended the call politely.

Lakiesha called to inquire about the status of a refund related to a plumbing service that had already been processed. The after-hours assistant documented her request and informed her that a team member would follow up on the next business day. The call ended with no further questions from Lakiesha.
The user called to speak with Danielle and requested to be transferred. The agent successfully transferred the call to the appropriate person.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.

Caleb called to schedule a routine heating and cooling tune-up through American Home Shield without a dispatch number. The agent confirmed the details and informed Caleb that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact him shortly. Caleb declined additional information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.

Fabiola from American Home Shield called Sophie at Budget regarding a dispatch service but did not specify the type of service before the call ended. The agent attempted to clarify the service type but the call was disconnected shortly after.

Edward Hopkins called to schedule a regular spring air conditioning cleaning for his home at 1 Moorbriar Ct, Saint Peters. The agent confirmed details and booked an appointment for Thursday at 12 PM. The user declined to provide feedback but was satisfied with the service.

Kenneth called to reschedule his air conditioning service appointment under his American Home Shield home warranty. The agent confirmed the details and dispatch number, and Kenneth declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests. The agent assured that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact him soon to finalize the rescheduling.
The call was very brief and disconnected shortly after the agent's initial greeting and request for the user's service address ZIP code. The user did not provide any information before hanging up.
The user requested to be transferred to a live representative named Gigi. The agent acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to the specified live agent.

The user called to confirm details about an air conditioner service appointment scheduled for the next day by 10 AM. The agent confirmed the service details and offered information about maintenance programs, which the user declined. The call ended with the agent assuring further contact from the service team.

The user called regarding an estimate but was unable to communicate effectively with the initial agent and requested to speak with a live human. The call was then transferred to another agent for further assistance.

The user provided a brief announcement about a tornado warning causing bus delays for the Hazelwood School District and gave instructions to opt out of future messages. The call ended shortly after the message was delivered.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The call was very brief and did not progress beyond initial greetings. The user identified themselves as an escalation specialist from American Home Shield, but no further conversation occurred.

The call was brief and involved an introduction exchange where the user identified themselves as an escalation specialist and asked for the agent's name and role. The agent responded with their name and role but no further conversation occurred before the user hung up.

The user wanted to speak directly with Cole regarding a discrepancy in a quote for an evaporator coil repair and requested a copy of the picture Cole took of the unit. The agent attempted to assist but ultimately transferred the call to another agent to handle the user's concerns. The call ended with a successful transfer.
The user requested to be connected to a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred to another agent. The initial agent facilitated the transfer promptly without any issues.
The call was very brief, with the user greeting the agent and the agent attempting to offer assistance, but the call ended abruptly shortly after.
The user requested to speak to a human agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to a live agent successfully. The call lasted 13 seconds and ended with a call transfer.
The user requested to speak with the manager of Budget Heating and Cooling, and the agent transferred the call to the appropriate person successfully.

The user, Brandy from Ferguson Plumbing Supply, called to report a delivery at Budget during a tornado. The agent collected delivery details including location, user role, and contact information. The call ended after confirming the delivery specifics without any issues.
The user called and requested to speak to a representative. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to the appropriate number successfully.

Mary Jo called to inquire about the time of a scheduled walkthrough on Thursday. The agent was unable to find the appointment in the system and offered to transfer her to a live representative, which she accepted. The call ended with a successful transfer to a live agent for further assistance.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user called from American Shield seeking information about a dispatch and requested to speak with a live representative. The virtual assistant, Sophie, confirmed she was not a live person and proceeded to transfer the call to a live agent. The call was successfully transferred to a live representative.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another agent successfully. The call lasted 17 seconds and ended with a call transfer.
The user requested to speak with a real live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a human agent as requested. The call ended with a successful transfer without further conversation.

Aaron Spencer from the Center for Autism Education called to report a loose toilet that needs to be securely fastened. The agent scheduled a service appointment for the next morning at 4140 Old Mill Parkway, Saint Peters, Missouri. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another representative successfully. The call lasted 15 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

The user, Jacob, called to discuss a voicemail from Amy regarding verifying his presence at home for a service appointment. He confirmed he is at home and mentioned he has a free tune-up through his home warranty. The call ended abruptly before scheduling the appointment.

Paul called to schedule a summer AC checkup appointment for the week of May 11th in the afternoon. The agent offered an available slot on May 11th at 8 AM, but Paul preferred an afternoon time. The call ended abruptly after Paul repeated "Hello?" and pressed digits.

John Heitman informed the agent about a rescheduled inspection appointment due to a tornado warning and city hall closure. The agent gathered details about the inspection, confirming it was a home warranty HVAC inspection for a new installation, and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Chris called to report an issue with his central air system that is running but not cooling the house. The agent collected necessary details including service address and confirmed the information with Chris. Chris expressed interest in home maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the call experience.

Russell called to update his contact information, providing his correct cell phone number to the agent. The agent confirmed the updated number and assured Russell that the information would be passed to the team. No service request was made during the call.

Steven called to change his plumbing appointment to install a new water heater on Friday, May 1st, between 8 AM and 12 PM. The agent confirmed the details and informed him that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact him soon. Steven declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

The user called to reschedule a preventative maintenance appointment for their air conditioning unit originally set for tomorrow, requesting a new appointment on Wednesday mornings. The service address was updated to 1520 W Bell, Swansea, IL 62226. The user declined information about maintenance programs and did not provide feedback.
The user requested to speak to a live representative and the agent successfully transferred the call to another agent. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.

Ali from American Home Shield contacted Budget to request the HVAC service report for dispatch number 34216099, with a technician visit on April 23rd. Ali requested the report be sent to their authorizations department as soon as possible, preferably the same day, and confirmed the correct service address and contact information. The call ended positively with Ali providing high feedback on the agent's assistance.

The user delivered a weather-related early dismissal announcement for Hazelwood School District. The agent then introduced herself and offered assistance, but the user ended the call immediately after.

Keisha called to reschedule her heating service appointment covered by American Home Shield. The agent confirmed the details and informed her that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact her shortly. Keisha declined information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the call experience.

The user repeatedly asked to speak with specific individuals (Stacy and Cece), but the agent was unable to connect them and kept repeating the greeting. The call ended abruptly after 29 seconds without resolution.
The call was very brief and disconnected shortly after the agent began speaking, with no interaction from the user.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and completed a successful call transfer.
